demographic_audit_note: Verified Medicare Advantage PPO; confirmed home address matches face sheet. Discovered an error in the emergency contact phone number in Atlantis and corrected it after speaking with the patient's daughter.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Home - SW confirmed the patient has a strong social support network and the goal is to return home with specialized PT for MS management.
stage_1_intake_responses: Q1: No, I haven't heard of you. Q2: To help me get settled at home? Q3: The social worker mentioned someone would help with the transition. Q4: I am worried about the timing of my IV steroid infusions and if I can manage the stairs; escalated mobility/medication concerns to SW.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Patient is completing IV steroid course; PT reports improved gait but remains at high fall risk. SW targets discharge for Friday.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card successfully inserted into patient's smartphone; program flyer delivered to daughter during bedside visit.
hha_confirmation_status: In Place - Initially 'Not In Place' due to Provider Illness (assigned MS-specialist nurse out with flu); investigated with HHA Intake and confirmed a backup nurse is assigned for Saturday morning SOC.
stage_3_followup_audit: Call 1 (to SW): Confirmed Friday D/C is firm and HHA has confirmed the Saturday 9:00 AM start-of-care. Call 2 (to family): Confirmed daughter understands the Saturday nurse arrival and has the walker ready.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached patient 24 hours prior to discharge; confirmed she has her new prescriptions and knows the MA will visit on Sunday.
atlantis_final_sync: Entered D/C date (11/15) and 1st visit date (11/16) into Atlantis system.
ma_visit_booking: MA visit scheduled for 11/16 at 2:00 PM; booking was held until HHA confirmed the backup nurse assignment following the primary provider's illness.
narrative_summary: Patient Elena Vance was triaged in Atlantis following an MS flare-up with a clear goal for home discharge. During the Connection Intake, she expressed anxiety regarding her IV steroid schedule, which was immediately escalated to the Social Worker. The transition faced a delay when the primary HHA nurse fell ill, but by adhering to the HHA-First Rule, I delayed MA scheduling until a backup nurse was confirmed 'In Place'. The Confidence Loop was closed through a final audit with the SW and family, ensuring a safe handoff for the Saturday start-of-care.

format_1_state_log.event_description: HHA intake coordinator reports the assigned MS-specialist nurse is out with flu.
PN investigates barrier and HHA identifies a backup generalist nurse to cover the Saturday SOC.
PN verifies HHA 'In Place' status and proceeds to schedule MA visit within 24 hours of SOC.

format_2_triples.situation: Patient expresses anxiety about IV infusion timing during Connection Intake Q4.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Escalated clinical concerns to the Social Worker for medication-schedule clarification.

format_2_triples.situation: HHA reports the assigned nurse is sick and cannot meet the Friday start-of-care.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Investigated the specific barrier with HHA Intake and held the MA schedule until a backup was assigned.
Performed Confidence Audit calls to both the SW and the patient's family to verify Day 1 alignment.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Wait for the Social Worker to notify the HHA about the nurse illness and provide a new discharge date before updating Atlantis.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the SW's role as the primary coordinator but delays the PN's ability to secure the MA visit window.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Call the HHA intake coordinator to identify the specific staffing blocker, categorize it as Provider Illness, and escalate the need for a backup nurse to the SW to keep the Friday discharge on track.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This follows the PN's 'Deep Dive HHA Barrier' checklist and ensures the HHA-First Rule is satisfied before scheduling the MA.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Call a different HHA agency to see if they have an available MS-specialist nurse and suggest to the SW that they switch the referral to the new agency.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Suggesting specific alternative HHAs to the SW is a Banned Action and an automatic overstep of the PN's boundary.
